behavior_summary: Defines Xavier NPC behavior: how he responds to being examined, how his routine/schedule is chosen and spawned, how he reacts to area entry/exit, theft, casting events, and simple item-search/encounter subroutines.

XAVIER::look: when a player 'looks' at Xavier, the script checks whether he is dead and produces an appropriate short bark. If alive it composes a name/job label based on two global flags (xavierName and xavierJob).
Scheduling: U8P_XAVIER::schedule queries system helpers to choose a schedule variant, writes xavierSchedule, and spawns asynchronous handlers to implement that schedule.

Area triggers: U8P_XAVIER::enterFastArea clears various reference slots and spawns the behavior matching xavierSchedule; U8P_XAVIER::leaveFastArea clears transient flags and, if the NPC is dead, teleports the corpse to random coordinates.

Reactive behaviors: U8P_XAVIER::AvatarStoleSomething pursues/reprimands the thief and spawns follow-up behavior; U8P_XAVIER::cast causes a flee/teleport sequence involving dialog, animation/frame changes, and clearing immortal state.
Auxiliary subroutines (U8P_XAVIER::0598, U8P_XAVIER::066F) implement item-search, pathfinding, greetings, and approach logic used by schedule-driven behaviors.
notable_control_flow: XAVIER::look branches on Npc::isDead: corpse description vs composed live identification.

U8P_XAVIER::schedule repeatedly queries FREE::28F9 to select a schedule variant and only writes xavierSchedule after spawn synchronization; schedule determines which sub-behavior will be spawned by enterFastArea.

U8P_XAVIER::enterFastArea unconditionally resets multiple reference slots (resetRef) before reading xavierSchedule and spawning one of three subroutines (schedule==1 -> 0BA6, ==2 -> 0598, ==3 -> 1429).
U8P_XAVIER::leaveFastArea clears xavierJustHealed and invokes FREE::2A56 for cleanup; if Xavier is dead it generates two random coordinates (urandom) and calls Npc::teleport to move the corpse.

U8P_XAVIER::cast waits/polls for a target (loop with countdown), then clears timers/refs, barks 'I must flee!', adjusts animation/frames and immortal/dead flags, and spawns timed actions (METHOD::15E7, METHOD::0113) to implement fleeing/teleport behavior.
open_questions: Exact meanings of schedule variants (values stored in xavierSchedule) beyond which handler they spawn—what game-time behaviors each schedule encodes remains inferred from spawn targets.
Precise semantics and side effects of free/system calls FREE::28F9, FREE::2A56, FREE::29D8, FREE::1C19 and METHOD::143A/METHOD::133F beyond their inferred roles as queries/timers/spawns.
What triggers U8P_XAVIER::cast in gameplay (player action, spell type, or scripted event) and the meaning/format of its implicit 'cast argument' parameter.
The concrete relationships between the item-search routine (U8P_XAVIER::0598) and world objects: which item shapes/frames are targeted and how prioritized selection works.

main_function.name: U8P_XAVIER::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to an examine/U8P_XAVIER::look event on the Xavier NPC. If Xavier is dead, present a corpse description; otherwise produce a short identification string composed from global flags (xavierName and xavierJob).
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_XAVIER::look) invoked when a player or actor examines Xavier (process invoked with viewer pid).

function_map.name: U8P_XAVIER::look
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_XAVIER::look' (examine) interactions: produce corpse or identification barks using xavierName/xavierJob globals.

function_map.name: U8P_XAVIER::schedule
function_map.role: Decide and persist Xavier's schedule variant (writes xavierSchedule) and spawn schedule-specific handlers.

function_map.name: U8P_XAVIER::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Area-entry trigger: reset refs and spawn the behavior corresponding to xavierSchedule.

function_map.name: U8P_XAVIER::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Area-exit trigger: cleanup refs and timers, clear xavierJustHealed, and teleport corpse if dead.

function_map.name: U8P_XAVIER::cast
function_map.role: Handle a casting-related event: wait for a target, then perform a flee sequence (dialog, animation/frame changes, clear immortal/set dead, teleport) with timing spawns.

function_map.name: U8P_XAVIER::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: React when the avatar steals: locate stolen item, set activity to approach, bark a reprimand, and spawn follow-up behavior.

function_map.name: U8P_XAVIER::0598
function_map.role: Item-search and pathfinding subroutine used by schedule 2 behavior to find and approach items, then spawn encounter handlers.

function_map.name: U8P_XAVIER::066F
function_map.role: Encounter/micro-behavior helper: greeting logic, conditional dialog (dependent on xavierMet), and small movement/approach decisions.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the item-search routine when xavierSchedule == 2.
Spawn schedule-specific handler when xavierSchedule == 1 (inferred name/label used in binary).
When an item/target is found and reachable, spawn the encounter/micro-behavior handler.
Spawn preliminary schedule activation/validation step before writing xavierSchedule.
Spawn the schedule enforcement/handler after xavierSchedule is set.

external_calls.purpose: Determine whether to show corpse description or live identification.
Produce short dialog/bark strings directed at the viewing player (e.g., 'the body of Xavier', 'Xavier the monk').
Clear reference/target slots so the NPC forgets previous targets when entering the fast area.
Query system/free helper repeatedly to decide schedule variant and spawn parameters.
Clear timers/counters/flags related to references upon leaving the area.
Generate random coordinates used to teleport a dead NPC's corpse to a random location.
Teleport Xavier's corpse to the coordinates produced by urandom.
Poll for the presence of a target before initiating flee/teleport behavior.
Make the NPC say 'I must flee!' as part of the cast reaction.
Clear immortal status and mark the NPC dead as part of the flee/teleport sequence (engine state changes).
Examine candidate items and pathfind toward reachable targets.
Produce greeting or helpful messages; behavior varies with xavierMet flag.

reads.name: xavierName
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether Xavier's personal name is included in the live identification bark.

reads.name: xavierJob
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether a job/title (e.g., 'monk' or 'man') is appended to Xavier's identification string.

reads.name: xavierSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Determines which behavioral subroutine to spawn when entering the fast area and gates certain reactions.

reads.name: xavierMet
reads.likely_effect: Toggles first-time vs repeat greeting dialog in encounter subroutines.

writes.name: xavierSchedule
writes.likely_effect: Persist the schedule variant chosen by U8P_XAVIER::schedule so other handlers (enterFastArea, AvatarStoleSomething) can act accordingly.

writes.name: xavierJustHealed
writes.likely_effect: Cleared on leaveFastArea to indicate the NPC is no longer in a transient 'just healed' state.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Defines Xavier NPC behavior: how he responds to being examined, how his routine/schedule is chosen and spawned, how he reacts to area entry/exit, theft, casting events, and simple item-search/encounter subroutines.
